mappings.rationale: Core text covering populations, communities, species interactions, ecosystems, ecological succession, and community structure (A, B, C).
Explains population genetics, natural selection, genetic drift, and the evolutionary dimensions of population interactions (A.2.a, B.3).
Directly covers mutualism, commensalism, and parasitic interactions as major forms of interspecific interaction (B.2.b, B.2.c).
Presents logistic growth models and Lotka-Volterra predator-prey equations that formalize population growth and species interaction dynamics (A.3, B.2).
Examines population dispersal, barriers to movement, and how introduced species disrupt community structure and native interactions (A.5, B.2, C.1).
Illustrates community structure, species diversity, niches, symbiotic interactions, and zonation in a complex marine community (C.1, C.2).
Illustrates vertical and horizontal stratification, community succession, niches, and species diversity in terrestrial communities (C.1.a, C.3, C.4).
Explores marine community structure, population dynamics, and ecological interactions within ocean ecosystems (C.1, C.5).
Addresses species diversity, community classification, and factors affecting population structure and biological processes (C.1.d, C.5).
Reveals microbial community structure, host-microbe symbiosis, and the dynamics of complex microbial populations (B.2.c, C.1.c).
Analyzes intraspecific competition for mates and sexual dimorphism as key forms of within-species population interaction (B.1).
Documents species loss and population collapse, shedding light on population fluctuations and community change over geological time (A.4, C.3).
Highlights plant diversity, pollination ecology, and plant-pollinator relationships as examples of interspecific mutualism (B.2.c).
Addresses genetic variation, sex linkage, and the Red Queen hypothesis relevant to population genetics and co-evolutionary interactions (A.2.a, B.3).
